zh.Wiggenhall, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得
zh.East Winch, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Cornwall於大約1285年出生在英國林可爾史爾諾可時，她的父親Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock34歲，母親Joan FitzAlan27歲。她於1309年與John Howard在英國史洛普爾布福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1341年在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年58歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

zh-hans.Wiggenhall, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得
zh-hans.East Winch, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Cornwall于大约1285年出生在英国林可爾史爾諾可时，她的父亲Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock34岁，母亲Joan FitzAlan27岁。她于1309年与John Howard在英国史洛普爾布福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1341年在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年58岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Cornwall was born about 1285, in Thonock, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, was 34 and her mother, Joan FitzAlan, was 27. She married John Howard in 1309, in Burford, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1341, in East Winch, Norfolk, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in East Winch, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Cornwall est née environ 1285, à Thonock, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzAlan, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé John Howard en 1309, à Burford, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1341, à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Cornwall um 1285 in Thonock, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock 34 und ihre Mutter Joan FitzAlan 27. Sie hat 1309 in Burford, Shropshire, England John Howard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1341 im Alter von 58 Jahren in East Winch, Norfolk, England und wurde in East Winch, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Cornwall è nata il circa 1285 nel Thonock, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Joan FitzAlan, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato John Howard nel 1309, nel Burford, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1341, nel East Winch, Norfolk, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolta a East Winch, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwallが約1285年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアソノックで生まれた時，父Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnockは34歳，母Joan FitzAlanは27歳であった。1309年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードでJohn Howardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1341年にイギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチで58歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwall(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 링코실르 토노c크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan FitzAlan(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1309년 영국 슈럽실르 버퍼드에서 John Howard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1341년 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에 묻혔다.

mg.Abt 1285–about 1341: tany ho any amin'ny 1285–tany ho any amin'ny 1341

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Cornwall nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Thonock, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Joan FitzAlan, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com John Howard em 1309, em Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1341, em East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Cornwall родилась около 1285 г. в местности тонокк, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzAlan, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Howard в 1309 г. в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1341 г. в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия.

sm.Abt 1285–about 1341: e tusa o le 1285–e tusa o le 1341

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Cornwall nació aproximadamente 1285, en Thonock, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Joan FitzAlan, tenía 27 años. Se casó con John Howard en 1309, en Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1341, en East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
